stem: A mission enters the space mission pipeline. The probability of passing design review is between 76% and 94% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing testing is 81%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing launch is 83%. What is the overall probability that the mission is successful?

ground_truth.answer: P(successful) ranges from 0.511 to 0.632 as the design review pass rate varies from 76% to 94%. Point estimate: 0.571.

solution_steps: The mission must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the design review stage creates a 12.1 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the design review rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
